recertificationReason: Stochastic-quality-sweep round 6 of 20 (trigger simplify, parent-expanded simplify-to-doc child workflow mode) deleted dead exported FolderToTopicMapping function from internal/connector/bookmarks/bookmarks.go and reconciled 8 stale planning-truth references in spec.md (4 sites) + design.md (4 sites) to name the actual production TopicMapper.MapFolder cascade. Per Gate G088 the planning-truth edits to the certified spec require a certifiedAt bump; this re-certification preserves originalCompletedAt (2026-04-09T01:40:00Z) and threads through prior intermediate re-cert timestamps. No user-facing behavior change; the dead utility had zero production consumers (proven by go build ./... exit 0). Pre-existing parent-spec gate failures (G060 TDD red-green markers, regression-E2E DoD items, consumer-trace DoD items, change-boundary DoD item, G053 code-diff-evidence, artifact-lint evidence-block thresholds) predate this round and are documented under observations as separate retrofit concerns; this round does NOT aim to remediate them.

executionHistory.summary: Created spec.md with full business analysis: 4 actors, 5 use cases, 10 BDD scenarios, outcome contract, competitive analysis, 5 improvement proposals, 13 requirements, folder-to-topic mapping architecture

Created design.md: wrapper pattern over existing bookmarks parsers, single-file connector.go + topics.go + dedup.go, no new NATS streams/migrations, auto-start registration in main.go, URL-based dedup, folder-to-topic hierarchical mapping, full processing tier for all bookmarks
Created scopes.md: 2-scope plan — Scope 1: Connector Implementation, Config & Registration; Scope 2: URL Dedup, Folder-to-Topic Mapping & Integration

Implemented both scopes: connector.go (442 lines), dedup.go (152 lines), topics.go (207 lines), connector_test.go (525 lines), dedup_test.go (157 lines), topics_test.go (74 lines). Registered in main.go with auto-start. Config added to smackerel.yaml.
All 26 unit tests pass across 3 test files (connector_test.go: 15 tests, dedup_test.go: 8 tests, topics_test.go: 3 tests). ./smackerel.sh test unit — bookmarks package ok 0.083s.
Regression verification complete. All 25 Go packages pass, 44 Python tests pass. No regressions in existing connector tests or pipeline tests.
Simplification pass completed. Reviewed connector.go, dedup.go, topics.go for unnecessary complexity. No dead code, no over-abstractions. Each file has clear single responsibility. No changes needed.
Gap analysis complete. All 10 Gherkin scenarios (SCN-BK-001 through SCN-BK-010) have mapped tests. All 2 scopes have DoD items covering all acceptance criteria. No orphaned scenarios.
Hardening pass completed. Reviewed spec/design/scopes for completeness. Gherkin scenarios well-formed. Test plan covers all scenarios. DoD items verifiable. No issues found.
Stabilization pass completed. All tests deterministic — no flaky failures. No time-dependent assertions. No external network calls in unit tests. Temp dirs cleaned up via t.TempDir().

Security review complete. Import directory validation prevents path traversal. Config uses SST pipeline — no hardcoded values. URL normalization strips tracking params without executing them. No SQL injection vectors (dedup/topics use parameterized queries).
Validation complete. ./smackerel.sh test unit — all 25 packages pass. ./smackerel.sh lint — exit 0. ./smackerel.sh check — config in sync. Both scopes Done with all DoD items verified.

Audit pass completed. Code follows existing connector patterns (Keep, Browser, Maps). No config SST violations. NATS contract not modified. No new migrations — uses existing tables. Docker Compose has bookmarks volume mount and env var.

Chaos pass completed. Corrupted file handling verified (TestSyncCorruptedFileSkipped, TestSyncCorruptedExportNoPanic). Empty import dir handled. Nil pool graceful degradation (TestFilterNew_NilPool, TestTopicMapper_NilPool). Invalid URL normalization (TestNormalizeURL_Regression_NoPanic).

Chaos R16 probe. Found and fixed: F-CHAOS-C16-001 (health state corruption on Sync() early exit before dedup — sync counters not flushed to struct before return), F-CHAOS-C16-002 (zero-bookmark files consumed silently without warning log). Added 4 adversarial regression tests: TestChaosC16_CancelledSyncWithDedupHealthError, TestChaosC16_CancelledDuringFileLoopWithDedup, TestChaosC16_NonBookmarkHTMLZeroArtifacts, TestChaosC16_PartialSuccessWithDedupHealthy. All 34+ bookmarks tests pass. Lint passes.

Chaos R24 probe. Found and fixed 3 findings: R24-001 (Sync reads c.config without lock — data race with concurrent Connect; snapshot cfg := c.config under lock, aligned with maps/browser/markets/alerts pattern), R24-002 (NormalizeURL preserves userinfo — credential leak into SourceRef/DB; u.User = nil, CWE-522), R24-003 (Chrome date_added uncapped far-future timestamps; added maxReasonableUnixSec year-2100 ceiling). Added 8 adversarial regression tests. All bookmarks tests pass. Lint passes.

Improve-existing sweep (stochastic-quality-sweep child). Found and fixed 2 findings: IMP-009-R-001 (ParseNetscapeHTML flat folder tracking lost nested hierarchy — replaced with stack-based DL/H3 tracking), IMP-009-R-002 (NormalizeURL missing www. prefix stripping — added 4-char prefix strip after host lowercasing). Added 8 adversarial regression tests across bookmarks_test.go and dedup_test.go. All 33 Go packages pass. Lint passes.
Documentation pass completed. report.md updated with test evidence, validation evidence, audit evidence, chaos evidence. scopes.md DoD items have evidence blocks. uservalidation.md updated.
Spec review completed. spec.md, design.md, scopes.md consistent. No stale or superseded content. All requirements traceable to implementation.
All unit tests pass (26 tests across 3 files), lint passes, format passes, check passes. Both scopes marked Done with verified DoD items. Delivery lockdown certification complete.

Gaps R2 sweep (stochastic-quality-sweep child). Revisited deferred GAP-004 from prior sweep. Found and fixed 3 findings: GAP-R2-001 (Health() never re-validates import dir existence post-Connect — added active os.Stat check), GAP-R2-002 (pendingFiles field missing from struct — added field and populated in findNewFiles), GAP-R2-003 (content_fetched metadata missing — added initial false value per R-006). Added 5 adversarial regression tests. All Go packages pass, 257 Python tests pass. Lint/check clean.

cross_spec_trace_guard_remediation_via_per_class_fixes (Iter 9): Repointed Test Plan rows T-2-13..T-2-17 to tests/integration/bookmarks_dedup_test.go and tests/integration/bookmarks_topics_test.go; T-2-18..T-2-19 to tests/e2e/test_bookmark_import.sh; tagged DoD bullets for SCN-BK-004/006/007/008 with 'Scenario SCN-BK-NNN (title):' prefix; appended trace-guard remediation section to report.md. trace-guard PASSED (0 warnings).

Stochastic-quality-sweep sweep-2026-05-23-r30 round 14, parent-expanded child workflow mode chaos-hardening. Chaos probe of NormalizeURL surfaced 3 defects (F-CHAOS-R30-001 control chars survive into SourceRef → PG insert failure/log injection/dedup miss [HIGH]; F-CHAOS-R30-002 default ports :80/:443/:21 not elided → dedup miss [MEDIUM]; F-CHAOS-R30-003 trailing DNS-root host dot not stripped → dedup miss [MEDIUM]). Spawned specs/009-bookmarks-connector/bugs/BUG-009-002-normalize-url-chaos-r30/ with full 6-artifact set; fixed all three in internal/connector/bookmarks/dedup.go (new stripURLControlChars helper + ordered host-canonicalisation block: trailing-dot strip → www. strip → default-port elision with IPv6 bracket re-add); added 6 adversarial regression tests in internal/connector/bookmarks/dedup_test.go (27 sub-cases total) with adversarial-fidelity proof (toggling the fix OFF causes 3 tests to FAIL with control-byte offset diagnostics; toggling it ON returns all to PASS). go vet clean, gofmt clean, full bookmarks package suite green.

Stochastic-quality-sweep sweep-2026-05-25-r10 round 10 (final), parent-expanded child workflow mode stabilize-to-doc. Stabilize probe of the bookmarks Sync() post-dedup topic-mapping loop surfaced 1 defect (F-STAB-R10-001 MEDIUM: loop ignored ctx.Err() between iterations, so a cancelled supervisor context burned one DB roundtrip per artifact each emitting its own stale slog.Warn — observable as O(N) post-shutdown warning storms on large bookmark exports). Spawned specs/009-bookmarks-connector/bugs/BUG-009-003-topic-mapping-cancel-r10/ with full 6-artifact set + scenario-manifest.json (3 scenarios). Fixed by extracting the 30-line inline loop into a new private method func (c *BookmarksConnector) mapFolderTopics(ctx context.Context, artifacts []connector.RawArtifact) int (internal/connector/bookmarks/connector.go) with a top-of-iteration ctx.Err() guard that emits exactly one structured slog.Warn("bookmarks topic mapping cancelled mid-loop", "error", err, "processed", processed, "remaining", len(artifacts)-processed) and returns processed. Sync() collapses to a single c.mapFolderTopics(ctx, allArtifacts) call. Added 3 adversarial regression tests (TestStabR10_TopicMappingRespectsContextCancel, TestStabR10_TopicMappingCancelBeforeFirstIteration, TestStabR10_TopicMappingNilMapper) in internal/connector/bookmarks/connector_test.go with adversarial-fidelity proof (toggling the ctx.Err() guard OFF causes the first two tests to FAIL with concrete diagnostics; restoring returns to PASS). go vet clean, gofmt clean, full bookmarks package suite green, cross-package consumer regression sweep across all 21 connector packages + scheduler PASS. artifact-lint PASS. state-transition-guard upgraded gates (G022/G053/G055/G056/G057/G060) retrofitted; one remaining BLOCK is the implementation-reality-scan FAKE_INTEGRATION heuristic false-positive on legitimate Go error-return idioms (return nil / return nil, err / return nil, fmt.Errorf), all flagged lines pre-existing per git blame, also surfaced by precedent BUG-009-002 + parent spec 009 closures — adopted precedent path per workflow-mode-resolution.md §recursive-tool blocker.

Stochastic-quality-sweep round 6 of 20 (trigger simplify, parent-expanded simplify-to-doc child workflow mode, statusCeiling done). Simplify probe of internal/connector/bookmarks/ (1276 source lines + 3825 test lines) surfaced 2 findings: F-SIMPLIFY-R6-001 (LOW: extractBookmarks 1-line shim wrapper with single caller — inlined directly in bookmarks.go; no bug spawned, unexported, no spec/design contract impact) and F-SIMPLIFY-R6-002 (MEDIUM: FolderToTopicMapping exported dead code + 8-site spec/design drift). For F-SIMPLIFY-R6-002 spawned specs/009-bookmarks-connector/bugs/BUG-009-004-foldertotopicmapping-dead-surface/ with full 7-artifact set (spec, design, scopes, scenario-manifest with 3 SCN-BK-FIX-004-NNN scenarios, report, uservalidation, state). Fix: deleted FolderToTopicMapping function from bookmarks.go (-10 lines) and 3 dedicated dead tests from bookmarks_test.go (-47 lines: TestFolderToTopicMapping, TestFolderToTopicMapping_Backslash, TestFolderToTopicMapping_MultiLevel); added TestSimplifyR6_FolderToTopicMapping_Removed in topics_test.go (+65 lines) with three layers of regression coverage (compile-pin on TopicMapper.MapFolder signature via direct call, runtime-pin on nil-pool early-return contract, inline algorithmic-intent reproduction of strings.Split + non-empty-trim loop). Rewrote 4 spec.md reference sites (Goal #2, ASCII diagram, R-005, UC-001 step 10) + 4 design.md reference sites (Design Brief, Component Design, Data Flow step 8, Component Design §2) to name TopicMapper.MapFolder. Total diff: 5 files, +78 insertions, -74 deletions, net +4 lines. Verification: go build ./... exit 0 (zero production consumers — strongest possible dead-code proof), bookmarks package suite 44 PASS / 0 FAIL with -race -count=1, consumer regression sweep across all 22 connector packages + scheduler green, go vet clean, gofmt clean, ./smackerel.sh lint exit 0. Synthetic adversarial proof captured showing 4 of 5 multi-segment fixtures distinguish flatten (1 segment) vs split (N segments) algorithms. Known scan disposition: G028 implementation-reality-scan flags 2 FAKE_INTEGRATION FPs at bookmarks.go:41/47 (return nil, fmt.Errorf inside ParseChromeJSON) — both lines pre-date simplify R6 and were NOT touched by this round; same disposition as R10 close-out (state.json executionHistory at 2026-05-25T17:30:00Z). G088 post-cert spec edit triggered by the 8 planning-truth rewrites in parent spec.md + design.md; remediated by certifiedAt bump from 2026-04-09T01:40:00Z (preserved as originalCompletedAt) to 2026-06-05T22:00:00Z.

OPS-002 G088 recert. Faithful cert ts was the prior recertedAt 2026-06-05T22:00Z; the only post-cert planning edit is bd72f76d (2026-06-06T04:38:31Z, 'refactor(sweep): connector simplifications, dead-code removal, test hardening'), which touched 009 design.md (9 lines) + spec.md (13 lines) ONLY (no scopes.md), reconciling the remaining stale FolderToTopicMapping() references (Goal #2, design Current/Target State, sync step 8) to the real production TopicMapper.MapFolder cascade in internal/connector/bookmarks/topics.go after deleting the dead exported helper (BUG-009-004). The folder->topic mapping product goal is preserved; only the named-helper attribution is corrected to match the implementation. Incidental reconciliation (category b): no requirement/scenario/DoD semantic change; behavior unchanged (bookmarks suite green per commit). certifiedAt advanced to 2026-06-06T17:00:00Z for Gate G088.

observations.summary: G028 implementation-reality-scan flags 2 pre-existing FAKE_INTEGRATION FPs at bookmarks.go:41 and bookmarks.go:47. Both lines are legitimate Go error-return idioms (`return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse Chrome JSON: %w", err)` and `return nil, fmt.Errorf("missing 'roots' in Chrome bookmarks")`) inside ParseChromeJSON that pre-date simplify R6 by many cycles (original 2026-04-09 certification). Same disposition as spec 009 R10 close-out (state.json executionHistory at 2026-05-25T17:30:00Z) and BUG-009-002 R30 close-out. Not introduced by this round; not a candidate for code change; documented disposition.

Adversarial guard test `TestSimplifyR6_FolderToTopicMapping_Removed` provides compile-pin + nil-pool runtime-pin + inline algorithmic-intent reproduction, but does NOT directly catch a hypothetical future internal flatten-rewrite of TopicMapper.MapFolder. That coverage gap is intentionally delegated to the integration test suite at `tests/integration/bookmarks_topics_test.go` (T-2-13..T-2-19 per parent scopes.md) which exercises the full DB-backed cascade with a live PostgreSQL pool. Honest scoping is documented in the bug's design.md → 'Adversarial Fidelity Protocol' → 'What the guard test does NOT catch'.

Parent spec 009 state-transition-guard re-run after R6 certifiedAt bump surfaces pre-existing gate failures: G060 (TDD red→green markers absent in original 2026-04-09 scopes.md), G022 (phase-claim provenance missing for analyze/bootstrap/plan in original execution history), regression-E2E DoD items missing on Scope 01/02 (gate added after original cert), consumer-trace planning DoD missing on Scope 02 (gate added after original cert), change-boundary DoD missing on scopes.md (gate added after original cert), G053 (Code Diff Evidence section absent in original report.md — gate added after original cert), G028 (FAKE_INTEGRATION FPs on legitimate Go error returns), 1 of 17 report.md evidence blocks below dual-terminal-signal threshold. ALL of these are pre-existing tech-debt accumulated since 2026-04-09 by newer gates being added to the framework. Simplify R6 did NOT introduce any of them; this round's surface is strictly limited to the 8 surgical reference-site rewrites in spec.md + design.md and the 3-file code change in internal/connector/bookmarks/. Retrofit of these older-cert gate failures is a separate concern that requires its own dedicated harden-to-doc or spec-scope-hardening cycle (analogous to the spec 080 R5 retrofit pattern at 2026-06-05T20:50:00Z). Tracked as observation; not a blocker for the R6 simplify deliverable.
observations.recommendedRemediation: Dedicated spec 009 harden-to-doc cycle to retrofit G060 markers, regression-E2E DoD items, consumer-trace DoD items, change-boundary DoD, Code Diff Evidence, and the artifact-lint evidence-block threshold.
